2605.00364.title: Unlearning What Matters: Token-Level Attribution for Precise Language Model Unlearning
2605.00364.paper_summary: Machine unlearning has emerged as a critical capability for addressing privacy, safety, and regulatory concerns in large language models (LLMs). Existing methods operate at the sequence level, applying uniform updates across all tokens despite only a subset encoding the knowledge targeted for removal. This introduces gradient noise, degrades utility, and leads to suboptimal forgetting. We propose TokenUnlearn, a token-level attribution framework that identifies and selectively targets critical tokens. Our approach combines knowledge-aware signals via masking, and entropy-aware signals to yield importance scores for precise token selection. We develop two complementary strategies: hard selection, applying unlearning only to high-importance tokens, and soft weighting, modulating gradient contributions based on importance scores. Both extend existing methods to token-level variants. Theoretical analysis shows token-level selection improves gradient signal-to-noise ratio. Experiments on TOFU and WMDP benchmarks across three model architectures demonstrate consistent improvements over sequence-level baselines in both forgetting effectiveness and utility preservation.
2605.00364.paper_summary_zh: 機器遺忘已成為解決大型語言模型（LLMs）中的隱私、安全和監管問題的關鍵能力。現有方法在序列層面運作，對所有標記應用統一更新，儘管只有一部分編碼了目標移除的知識。這引入了梯度噪聲，降低了效用，並導致次優的遺忘。我們提出了TokenUnlearn，一個標記級的歸因框架，能夠識別並選擇性地針對關鍵標記。我們的方法結合了通過遮罩獲得的知識感知信號和熵感知信號，以產生精確標記選擇的重要性分數。我們開發了兩種互補策略：硬選擇，僅對高重要性標記應用遺忘，以及軟加權，根據重要性分數調節梯度貢獻。這兩者將現有方法擴展到標記級變體。理論分析顯示，標記級選擇改善了梯度信號與噪聲的比率。在TOFU和WMDP基準上對三種模型架構的實驗顯示，在遺忘有效性和效用保留方面，相較於序列級基準有一致的改善。
2605.00364.author: Jiawei Wu et.al.
